description_long: This is an old, weathered bucket with a cylindrical, slightly tapered body and a rounded rim. The surface is predominantly a dark reddish-brown with patches of greenish corrosion and visible wear, suggesting it is made from leather or a similar material, possibly with some metal reinforcement. The bucket features a thick, twisted bail handle attached to metal loops on either side of the rim, showing signs of rust and age. One side of the bucket displays faded white painted text or markings, adding to its vintage character. The interior appears rough and stained, consistent with prolonged use and exposure to the elements. This bucket would likely be found in historical or utilitarian urban settings, such as old fire stations, stables, or workshops.
description: A weathered, reddish-brown bucket with a thick twisted handle, visible corrosion, and faded white markings, showing signs of age and heavy use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the bucket's rounded rim, thick twisted handle, and faded white painted text on the side.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the bucket's cylindrical shape, worn surface, and the handle's attachment to the rim.
description_view_2: Angled rear view displaying the bucket's taper, rough texture, and the handle's curve over the rim.
description_view_3: Oblique view emphasizing the faded white markings and the weathered, corroded surface.
